view.s368.s382.bankruptcy_exception: § 382(l)(5): G reorg in bankruptcy — historic creditors as continuing shareholders
view.s368.s382.built_in_gain: RBIG (recognized built-in gain) adjustments increase limit during 5-yr period
view.s368.s382.limit: Annual NOL limit: target value × federal long-term tax-exempt rate (~4-5%)
view.s368.s382.notice2003_65: Notice 2003-65 + 2019 proposed regs detail built-in gain measurement
view.s368.s382.trigger: Ownership change: > 50 pct-point shift over 3-yr test period among 5% shareholders
view.s36b.h1.title: // § 36B ACA PREMIUM TAX CREDIT
view.s36b.hint.intro: Refundable credit to reduce monthly health insurance premiums on Healthcare.gov. Pre-IRA: available only 100-400% FPL (cliff). ARPA / IRA 2022-2025: extended to ALL incomes when premium > 8.5% of MAGI. Reconciled on Form 8962. Critical for self-employed traders — losing one year's APTC due to high gain costs thousands. 2026 sunset: 400% FPL cliff returns.
view.s36b.plan.advance: If you'll exceed advance estimate, ACT NOW to drop MAGI
view.s36b.plan.cap_repayment: Pre-ARPA: repayment cap 200% FPL — limit repayment risk
view.s36b.plan.cliff_2026: 2026 cliff returns: get below 400% FPL or lose entire subsidy
view.s36b.plan.harvesting: Loss harvest to offset realized gains pre-year-end
view.s36b.plan.roth_timing: Time Roth conversions for low-income years
view.s36b.plan.solo_401k: Solo 401(k) max contribution to drop MAGI below cliff
view.s36b.warning.repay: Advance PTC exceeds earned PTC → you OWE the difference back on Form 8962. Repayment caps apply < 400% FPL. ≥ 400% FPL (pre-IRA) = full repayment.
view.s38.carry_note: Unused credit carries back 1 yr (file Form 1045 / 1139 quick refund) OR forward 20 yrs. For IRA 2022 specified credits: consider § 6417 direct pay (tax-exempts) or § 6418 transferability (taxables) to monetize immediately at 88-95¢ on $1.
view.s38.comp.alcohol: § 40 Alcohol fuel mixtures + § 40A biodiesel + § 40B Sustainable Aviation Fuel
view.s38.comp.employer: § 45E Small Employer Pension Setup + § 45F Childcare + § 45R Health Insurance
view.s38.comp.investment: Investment credit § 46: § 48 ITC, § 48C, § 48E, § 47 rehab, § 48D semi mfg
view.s38.comp.renewable: § 45 Renewable Electricity PTC + § 45Y Clean Electricity PTC
view.s38.comp.research: § 41 Research credit (incremental QRE method or ASC)
view.s38.comp.work_opportunity: § 51 Work Opportunity Tax Credit (WOTC): up to $9,600 per qualified hire
view.s38.h1.title: // § 38 GENERAL BUSINESS CREDIT
view.s38.hint.intro: Aggregator of business credits: § 41 R&D + § 45 PTC + § 48 ITC + § 45Q + § 30D + § 45W + § 45L + § 45X + Empowerment Zone + WOTC + Disabled Access + many more. Limit: net regular tax above TMT (Tentative Minimum Tax) + 25% of next $25K. Carryback 1-yr, Carryforward 20-yr (§ 39). Forms 3800 + 8000-series source forms. § 6417 direct pay + § 6418 transferability for IRA 2022 specified credits (different rules per credit).
view.s38.lim.25pct: + 25% of remaining tax above $25K (after TMT reached)
view.s38.lim.basic: Limit = Net Tax (regular) above Tentative Minimum Tax (TMT)
view.s38.lim.carry_back: Unused: carry back 1 yr first, then 20-year carryforward
view.s38.lim.eligible_small: Eligible small biz: R&D + energy + various credits can offset AMT entirely
view.s38.lim.pre_acquisition: § 39(d) pre-acquisition GBCs: SRLY-limited similar to NOL
view.s38.lim.s39_expiration: § 39 carryforward expires after 20 yrs → permanent loss
view.s38.lim.specified: 'Specified credits' (R&D portion, energy ITC, etc.) can offset AMT + TMT
view.s382.h1.title: // § 382 NOL LIMITATION (OWNERSHIP CHANGE)
view.s382.hint.intro: Limits NOL utilization after ownership change to annual limit = FMV × LT tax-exempt rate. § 382(h) RBIG/RBIL 5-yr. § 382(c) COBE 2-yr or NOLs eliminated.
view.s401a9.ex.first_year: First-year RMD can be delayed to April 1 of following year (but creates double RMD year)
view.s401a9.ex.inherited: Inherited IRA: separate rules (10-yr rule + EDB exceptions)
view.s401a9.ex.qcd_offset: QCD up to $105k offsets RMD income inclusion
view.s401a9.ex.roth_ira: Roth IRA: NO RMD during owner's life (SECURE 2.0 eliminated Roth 401(k) RMD too)
view.s401a9.ex.still_working: Still working + < 5% owner: 401(k) RMD deferred at current employer
view.s401a9.ex.terminal: Terminal illness: SECURE 2.0 exception (lifetime expectancy < 7 yrs)
view.s401a9.h1.title: // § 401(a)(9) RMD CALCULATOR
view.s401a9.hint.intro: SECURE Act 2019 + SECURE 2.0 2022 changed RBD (Required Beginning Date): Born ≤ 6/30/1949: age 70½. Born 7/1/1949 → 12/31/1950: age 72. Born 1951-1959: age 73. Born 1960+: age 75. RMD = prior-year Dec 31 balance ÷ life-expectancy divisor (Uniform Lifetime Table). QCD up to $105,000 (2024) from IRA offsets RMD without inclusion in income. Penalty for missed RMD: 25% (10% if timely corrected).
view.s401a9.note.joint: Spouse > 10 yrs younger sole beneficiary: use Joint Life Expectancy Table (longer divisor → smaller RMD). Recalculate annually.
view.s401a9.qcd.501c3: Direct transfer to 501(c)(3) public charity only (not DAF, foundation)
view.s401a9.qcd.cgi: Counts toward RMD if RMD age
view.s401a9.qcd.cgi_2024: $105,000 limit 2024 (indexed); SECURE 2.0 split $50k one-time CRT / CGA
view.s401a9.qcd.medicare: Lower AGI reduces Medicare IRMAA premiums (2-yr lookback)
view.s401a9.qcd.muni: Lower AGI keeps muni bond / Social Security from being taxed
view.s401a9.qcd.no_agi: Excludes amount from AGI — better than itemized charitable deduction
view.s401a9.qcd.over_70: Available age 70½+ even if RMD not yet required
view.s401a9.qcd.spouse: Each spouse has separate limit if separate IRAs
view.s401h.doc.affirmation: Post-2020 SECURE Act: employee self-certification accepted instead of docs
view.s401h.doc.funeral: Funeral: itemized funeral bills + death certificate
view.s401h.doc.home: Home purchase: P&S agreement, closing statement
view.s401h.doc.medical: Medical: itemized bills, doctor's letter, insurance EOBs
view.s401h.ex.domestic_abuse: Domestic abuse victim (lesser of $10k or 50% balance)
view.s401h.h1.title: // § 401(k) HARDSHIP WITHDRAWAL
view.s401h.hint.intro: 'Immediate + heavy financial need' + 'necessary' + 'no other resources'. Safe-harbor categories: medical, principal residence purchase, post-secondary education, prevent eviction / foreclosure, funeral, principal residence damage. SECURE 2.0 improvements: Roth source allowed + $1k emergency / yr + terminal illness + domestic abuse + disaster relief + employer match + own contributions all eligible.
view.s408a.h1.title: // § 408A ROTH IRA
view.s408a.hint.intro: After-tax contributions + tax-free qualified distributions. 2024 limits $7K / $8K catch-up. MAGI phase-out $146-161K single / $230-240K MFJ. Backdoor + mega backdoor.
view.s408d3.h1.title: // § 408(d)(3) BOBROW IRA ROLLOVER
view.s408d3.hint.intro: Bobrow v. Comm'r (2014): one indirect rollover per 12 months applies PER TAXPAYER across ALL your IRAs (Traditional + Roth + SEP + SIMPLE). Pre-Bobrow misreading allowed per-account. Second indirect rollover within 12 mo: amount taxable + 10% penalty if < 59½ + excess contribution + 6% excise. Trustee-to-trustee transfers do NOT count — unlimited. Roth conversions don't count.
view.s408d3.warning.violation: Multiple indirect rollovers within 12 months — second + later treated as TAXABLE DISTRIBUTION + possible 10% penalty + 6% excess contribution excise. File Form 5329 + consider self-certification.
view.s408d3.work.60_days: If you do indirect: 60 days strict; banks must withhold 20% (less for IRA)
view.s408d3.work.conversions: Roth conversions don't count toward the limit
view.s408d3.work.direct: Always do direct trustee-to-trustee transfer — unlimited per year
view.s408d3.work.private_letter: PLR for other reasons — $10k+ user fee
view.s408d3.work.self_certify: Late rollover relief: Rev. Proc. 2016-47 self-certification (11 reasons)
view.s408d3.work.spousal_separate: Spouse's IRA rollovers count separately (each spouse has own limit)
view.s409A.dist.cic: Change in control — § 409A specific definitions (not always M&A)
view.s409A.dist.emergency: Unforeseeable emergency — limited; no medical / casualty insurance
view.s409A.dist.specified_employee: SPECIFIED EMPLOYEE (public co): 6-month delay on separation distributions
view.s409A.dist.time: Specified time or schedule — must be objectively determinable
view.s409A.exc.contractor: Non-employee director / contractor with multiple unrelated customers
view.s409A.exc.foreign: Foreign plans of bona fide foreign nationals (limited carve-out)
view.s409A.exc.separation_safe_harbor: Separation pay: 2-yr cap + 2× FICA wages limit ($330K × 2 = $660K)
view.s409A.exc.short_term: Short-term deferral: paid within 2.5 months of vesting
view.s409A.exc.stock_rights: Stock rights: at-the-money options + RSUs (settled when vest)
view.s409A.exc.welfare: Welfare benefit plans (group health, life, disability)
view.s409A.h1.title: // § 409A NONQUAL DEFERRED COMP
view.s409A.hint.intro: Defers tax on comp not actually / constructively received. VIOLATION: ALL deferred amounts taxed in current year + 20% additional tax + underpayment interest at 1% above market rate. 6 permitted distribution events: separation, disability, death, time/schedule, change in control, unforeseeable emergency. Initial election: 30 days post-eligibility OR before year start. Subsequent deferrals: 1-yr advance + 5-yr push-out. Specified employee (public co): 6-month delay on separation pay. Form W-2 box 12 code Y/Z.
view.s409A.pen.aggregation: Aggregation rules: all plans of same type aggregated for violation
view.s409A.pen.all_deferred: ALL deferred amounts taxable in year of violation (not just current vested)
view.s409A.pen.amended_return: Amended return required for corrected violations
view.s409A.pen.california: California: 20% state + interest stacks on top (parallel state law)
view.s409A.pen.correction: Notice 2010-6 + 2008-113: limited correction safe harbors (some violations)
view.s409A.pen.interest: + Interest at underpayment rate + 1% from year of deferral
view.s409A.pen.no_employee_relief: No relief for employee even if employer's fault
view.s409A.viol.acceleration: Acceleration of distribution to event other than 6 permitted
view.s409A.viol.cic_definition: Inaccurate change in control definition (must match § 409A regs)
view.s409A.viol.discount: Discounted stock options: strike < FMV = built-in deferred comp
view.s409A.viol.late_change: Late change of distribution timing without 1-yr advance + 5-yr push-out
view.s409A.viol.no_election: Failure to make timely initial election
view.s409A.viol.no_six_month: Specified employee separation pay distributed before 6-month delay
view.s409A.viol.severance: Severance > safe harbor without 6-month delay (public co)
view.s409A.violation_note: § 409A violation triggers ALL deferred amounts current taxation + 20% penalty + interest. Effective tax rate often EXCEEDS 60%. Consider Notice 2010-6 / 2008-113 correction safe harbors for operational failures. Document failure prevention via plan administration audit.
view.s41.h1.title: // § 41 RESEARCH CREDIT
view.s41.hint.intro: Regular method: 20% × (Current QREs − Base Amount). Base = avg gross receipts × fixed base %. ASC § 41(c)(5): 14% × (Current QREs − 50% × avg prior 3 yrs). Payroll tax election: qualified small biz (≤ $5M current revenue + ≤ 5 yrs revenue) uses up to $500K credit against employer FICA (PATH Act 2015). QREs: wages + supplies + contract research (65%) + computer time-share. § 174: 2022+ mandatory 5-yr / 15-yr amortization replaces immediate expensing.
view.s41.qre.basic_research: Basic research: separate § 41(b)(3) treatment — credit at 20% direct
view.s41.qre.computer: Computer time-share: cost of cloud computing + hosted services for qualified research
view.s41.qre.contract: Contract research: 65% of payments to contractors (75% for qualified energy research consortium)
view.s41.qre.no_capital: Cost of fixed assets NOT QRE — depreciation already deducted under § 168
view.s41.qre.no_funded: Funded research (paid by 3rd party) NOT QRE for performer
view.s41.qre.no_overhead: Overhead, indirect costs, depreciation NOT QRE
view.s41.qre.supplies: Supplies: tangible property used in research (NOT capital assets > 1 yr)
view.s41.qre.wages: Wages: W-2 Box 1 of employees engaged in qualified research (direct + supervision + support)
view.s41.qsb_note: Qualified small business: up to $500K of credit applied against employer FICA (6.2% OASDI portion) via Form 8974 + Form 941 quarterly. Valuable for pre-revenue startups with no income tax liability. Carryforward 5 years.
view.s41.s280c.basic: § 280C(c): deduction reduced by credit amount (no double benefit)
view.s41.s280c.bill_pending: Various proposals (TCDTRA, etc.) would restore § 174 immediate expensing
view.s41.s280c.coordination: Coordinate with § 174: § 280C applies to current QRE, but § 174 amortization spreads
view.s41.s280c.reduced: § 280C(c)(2) reduced credit election: take 79% credit but no deduction reduction (21%-rate)
view.s41.s280c.tcja: TCJA pre-2022: § 174 immediate expense; post-2022: forced amortization → credit value rises relative to deduction
view.s41.tst.all_four: ALL FOUR required — fact-intensive determination, contemporaneous documentation critical
view.s41.tst.documentation: Document: project objectives, technical challenges, alternatives evaluated, test results, time tracking
view.s41.tst.exclusions: Exclusions § 41(d)(4): post-commercial production, adaptation, duplication, surveys, foreign research, software for internal use (3-part test)
view.s41.tst.experimentation: PROCESS OF EXPERIMENTATION: evaluate alternatives via systematic process (hypothesis → test → analyze)
view.s41.tst.permitted_purpose: PERMITTED PURPOSE: new / improved business component (function, performance, reliability, quality)
view.s41.tst.tech_uncertainty: TECHNOLOGICAL UNCERTAINTY: develop info to eliminate uncertainty about capability / method / design
view.s41.tst.technological: TECHNOLOGICAL IN NATURE: rely on hard sciences (engineering, physics, biology, computer science, chemistry)
view.s412.at_risk_note: AT-RISK STATUS: § 430 load factor adds 40% to target normal cost. § 436 benefit restrictions: no lump sums (< 80%); no future accruals (< 60%); 50% lump sum available at 60-80%. Notify participants. Address shortfall via: increase contributions, plan freezes, benefit formula reduction (no clawback of accrued).
view.s412.atr.benefit_restrictions: § 436 benefit restrictions: no lump sums, no accruals if < 60% funded
view.s412.atr.computation: At-risk computation: accelerated assumptions + load factor
view.s412.atr.continue: Continued at-risk: 3 consecutive years → 'at-risk' load factor permanently
view.s412.atr.exemption: Recent shortfall exemption: temporary relief in plan years 2014-2024 (extension acts)
view.s412.atr.notice: Notice to participants: required when in at-risk + benefit restrictions
view.s412.atr.test: Test: Funded Target % < 80% AND Funded % AT-RISK < 70%
view.s412.atr.transition: Transition: gradual rule application for newly at-risk plans
view.s412.exc.contribution_deductibility: Tax deduction available for contributions (subject to § 404 limit)
view.s412.exc.cure_period: Cure period: usually 90 days to cure with payment
view.s412.exc.disqualification: Beyond cure: § 401(a) plan disqualification = all contributions taxable
view.s412.exc.fiduciary: Fiduciary liability: trustees may be personally liable
view.s412.exc.minimum: Reportable on Form 5330 separately from Form 5500
view.s412.exc.tier_1: Tier 1: 10% of accumulated funding deficiency annually
view.s412.exc.tier_2: Tier 2: 100% if not corrected within taxable period
view.s412.exc.waiver: § 412(c) waiver: temporary funding waiver (rare, limited duration)
view.s412.h1.title: // § 412 PENSION FUNDING
view.s412.h2.s4971: § 4971 excise tax for failure to fund
view.s412.hint.intro: DB plans must satisfy minimum funding to maintain qualified status under § 401(a). § 430 single-employer / § 431 multi-employer. Funding shortfall: triggers PBGC variable-rate premium + § 4971 excise tax (10% / 100% cumulative). Quarterly contributions required if shortfall + at-risk status. At-risk: < 80% / 70% funded → benefit restrictions + accelerated contributions. PBGC premium: $101 flat + 5.25% variable on shortfall (2025).
view.s412.mrc.at_risk: At-risk: additional load factor on target normal cost
view.s412.mrc.due_date: Quarterly due dates: 15 days after quarter end
view.s412.mrc.mrc: MRC = normal cost + shortfall amortization + at-risk add-on
view.s412.mrc.normal: Target Normal Cost: PV of benefits expected to accrue this year
view.s412.mrc.quarterly: Quarterly contributions required if shortfall exists
view.s412.mrc.target: Funding Target: PV of benefits accrued to date
view.s412.pbgc.form_5500: Form 5500 includes PBGC premium worksheet (Schedule SB)
view.s412.pbgc.partition: SFA (Special Financial Assistance) Act 2021: $86B grant to underfunded multi-employer plans
view.s412.pbgc.variable: Variable-rate premium: 5.25% × unfunded vested benefits (capped at $652/participant)
view.s414.aggregated_note: AGGREGATED for § 414: benefit plan coverage testing must include ALL group employees. § 401(k) deferral limit shared across employers. § 415 retirement plan limit per group. Top-heavy testing across group. ACA 50+ FTE: aggregated. § 409A specified employee determined at group level. Form 5500 may need single filing or coordinated.
view.s414.ap.aca_50: ACA: aggregate 50+ FTE employer mandate trigger
view.s414.ap.compensation: § 401(a)(17) comp limit ($345K 2024) — per group not per entity
view.s414.ap.coverage: § 410(b) plan coverage test (ratio percentage / average benefits)
view.s414.ap.s401_k: § 401(k) deferral limit ($23,500 2025) — per individual across all employers in group
view.s414.ap.s409a: § 409A deferred comp: timing rules + 6-month delay for specified employees
view.s414.ap.s415: § 415 retirement plan limit ($69K 2024 DC, $275K DB) — per group
view.s414.asg.a_org: A-Org: B-org provides services FOR FSO; B-org owners involved
view.s414.asg.b_org: B-Org: ≥ 10% owned by FSO owners; provides services TO FSO
view.s414.asg.complex: Complex test — IRS Form 5300 PLR for confirmation
view.s414.asg.fso: First Service Org (FSO): performs services for / with B-org
view.s414.asg.law_firms: Law firms / accountants: similar professional service organization rules
view.s414.asg.management: Management company test: B-org primarily manages FSO
view.s414.asg.medical: Medical practices: separate professional corps + management LLC = ASG
view.s414.asg.purpose: Captures shared service arrangements not caught by controlled group
view.s414.bs.5_taken_with: 'Taken together w/ each other' 5 or fewer + 80% + 50% identical
view.s414.bs.example: Example: A owns 60% Corp 1 + 30% Corp 2; B owns 40% Corp 1 + 70% Corp 2 → not controlled (only 30% identical for A, 40% for B)
view.s414.bs.identical: Identical: smallest interest each owner has in any corp
view.s414.bs.individuals_estates_trusts: Individuals, estates, trusts only (not corporate owners)
view.s414.bs.spouse: Spouses: constructive ownership unless legally separated
view.s414.bs.test_5: Test: 5 or fewer common shareholders own ≥ 80% of each corp
view.s414.bs.test_50: AND those 5 or fewer common shareholders have ≥ 50% IDENTICAL ownership
view.s414.h1.title: // § 414 AGGREGATED EMPLOYER
view.s414.hint.intro: § 414(b) controlled group: parent-subsidiary (80%+ ownership) OR brother-sister (5 or fewer common owners; 80%+ common + 50%+ identical). § 414(c): non-corporate trades / businesses under common control (LLCs, partnerships, sole props). § 414(m) affiliated service group: shared services + first-service org rules. Purpose: mandatory aggregation for benefit plan testing, retirement limits, ACA, deferred comp. Form 5500 + benefit coverage tests (§ 410(b), § 401(a)(4), § 416 top-heavy).
view.s414.ps.chain: Chain: A owns 80% of B owns 80% of C → A, B, C aggregated
view.s414.ps.exempt: Excluded: certain regulated investment companies, REITs, others
view.s414.ps.intermediary: Intermediary: stock of subs aggregated to identify common owner
view.s414.ps.measurement: Measurement: voting power AND value (both required)
view.s414.ps.ss_employee: Direct ownership counts; constructive ownership often controlling
view.s414.ps.test: Test: at least one corp owns ≥ 80% of another → controlled group
view.s414v.h1.title: // § 414(v) CATCH-UP CONTRIBUTIONS
view.s414v.hint.intro: Age 50+: $7,500 catch-up (2024) on top of $23k base 401(k). SECURE 2.0 Super Catch-up 60-63: $11,250. IRA: $1,000. SECURE 2.0 Mandatory Roth Catch-up for high earners (> $145k 2023 wages) → catch-up MUST be Roth (effective 2026 after delay). Plan must offer Roth option to provide catch-up to high earners.
view.s414v.rm.effective: Originally effective 2024; delayed by IRS Notice 2023-62 to 2026
view.s414v.rm.first_year: First year of employment: no Roth mandate (no prior-year wages)
view.s414v.rm.must_be_roth: Catch-up MUST be Roth (post-tax) — no pre-tax option
view.s414v.rm.no_option: No Roth option = no catch-up for high earners
view.s414v.rm.plan_offers: Plan must offer Roth option to provide catch-up to high earners
view.s414v.rm.simple_ira: SIMPLE IRA: Roth option since SECURE 2.0
view.s414v.rm.wages_only: 'Wages' = FICA wages from § 3121(a); SE income excluded
view.s414v.strat.full_calendar: Catch-up fills last; max base first
view.s414v.strat.no_match: Catch-up usually NOT matched by employer
view.s414v.strat.roth_for_high: High earners with Roth catch-up: locks in tax-free growth at peak earnings
view.s414v.strat.special_457b: § 457(b)(3) special last-3-year catch-up: 2× regular limit (use OR § 414(v) age 50, not both)
view.s414v.strat.spouse: Spousal IRA catch-up: each spouse $1,000 separately
view.s414v.warning.blocked: High earner but employer plan doesn't offer Roth — NO catch-up available starting 2026 unless plan adds Roth option.
view.s415.agg.affiliated: Affiliated service group: management or service relationship + ownership / common service
view.s415.agg.brother_sister: Brother-sister: 5 or fewer owners + each control 80% combined + 50% identical
view.s415.agg.controlled: Controlled group (parent-sub 80%+ OR brother-sister 80% identical owners): treated as ONE employer for § 415
view.s415.agg.self_employed: Self-employed + W-2 job: separate; can max both but 402(g) deferral shared
view.s415.agg.unrelated: Unrelated employers: SEPARATE $69k limits (employee can stack but $23k 402(g) combined)
view.s415.h1.title: // § 415 401(k) COMBINED LIMITS
view.s415.hint.intro: 2024 annual additions: $69,000 (employee + employer + after-tax) per individual per employer. § 415(b) Defined benefit: $275,000 annual benefit cap. Comp cap (§ 401(a)(17)): $345,000. HCE threshold (§ 414(q)): $155,000. 457(b) stacks on 401(k) limit (separate $23k). Multiple unrelated employers: separate $69k limits but combined employee deferral $23k.
view.s416.h1.title: // § 416 TOP-HEAVY PLAN
view.s416.hint.intro: Plan is top-heavy if KEY EMPLOYEES own > 60% of plan balance. Key employee: officer earning > $220k (2024), > 5% owner, OR > 1% owner earning > $150k. Top-heavy minimum: 3% non-key employee contribution. Faster vesting: 3-year cliff OR 2-6 year graded. Safe Harbor 401(k): deemed not top-heavy + skips ACP/ADP testing.
view.s416.ke.former_keys: Former key employees still counted for 5-yr lookback
view.s416.ke.officers_count: Officers: only top 50 by salary count (capped)
view.s416.r.401k_match_only: 401(k) with no employer contributions in top-heavy year
view.s416.r.aggregate: Aggregation rules: merge multiple plans to dilute key % below 60%
view.s416.r.distributions: Distributions to key employees count IF within last 5 years
view.s416.r.qaca: QACA (Qualified Auto Contribution Arrangement) — also relief
view.s416.r.safe_harbor: Adopt Safe Harbor 401(k) — deemed not top-heavy
view.s416.r.simple: SIMPLE plans: not subject to top-heavy
view.s416.v.2_6_graded: 2-6 year graded: 20% per year starting year 2 → 100% year 6
view.s416.v.faster_than_normal: Compare to 5-yr cliff OR 3-7 year graded for non-top-heavy
view.s416.v.match: Employer matching contributions: separate vesting (2-6 year graded since EGTRRA)
view.s416.v.safe_harbor: Safe Harbor: 100% immediate vesting of safe harbor contributions
view.s42.avg_note: Income averaging (post-2017 PATH Act): provides FLEXIBILITY to serve mixed-income populations. Average AMI ≤ 60% allows units up to 80% AMI to subsidize units at 20% AMI. Available for compliance period 2018+; required Treasury final regs Oct 2022.
view.s42.comp.5_year: First 5 years: heavier penalties; years 11-15 lighter
view.s42.comp.cure_period: 90-day cure period for noncompliance — fix before annual report
view.s42.comp.exception: Recapture exceptions: casualty, partner death, change of qualified basis (de minimis)
view.s42.comp.exit_strategy: Exit strategy: year 15 dispose to sponsor / nonprofit + Right of First Refusal
view.s42.comp.next_available: Next Available Unit Rule (§ 42(g)(2)(D)): rent next available to qualified tenant if existing falls out of compliance
view.s42.comp.recapture_pct: Recapture: 1/3 of credit claimed for each year out of compliance + interest
view.s42.comp.recertification: Annual tenant recertification — verify income still meets test
view.s42.comp.s42j: Annual Form 8609 + Schedule A by state HFA
view.s42.h1.title: // § 42 LIHTC
view.s42.hint.intro: 4% credit (bond-financed) OR 9% credit (competitive allocation by state HFA). 10-year credit period; 15-year compliance; 30+ year extended use agreement. Threshold tests: (1) 20-50 (≥ 20% units at ≤ 50% AMI), (2) 40-60 (≥ 40% at ≤ 60%), (3) post-2017 income averaging (≥ 40% at avg ≤ 60% AMI; units range 20-80%). Recapture 1/3 over 15 yrs on noncompliance. QCT / DDA bonus +30% qualified basis. Form 8609.
view.s42.str.15yr: 15-year compliance period: maintain affordability or recapture
view.s42.str.30yr: 30-year extended use: state agreement, includes early restrictions
view.s42.str.4pct: 4% credit: tax-exempt bond-financed (≥ 50% costs from bonds)
view.s42.str.9pct: 9% credit: NEW construction / substantial rehab (rehab ≥ $7K/unit + 10% basis)
view.s42.str.partner_partnership: Typical structure: 99.99% investor + 0.01% sponsor in Limited Partnership
view.s42.str.qap: State Qualified Allocation Plan (QAP): scoring for competitive 9%
view.s421.h1.title: // § 421 QUALIFIED STOCK OPTIONS (ISO + ESPP)
view.s421.hint.intro: § 422 ISO no exercise income + 2yr/1yr holding for LTCG + AMT trap. § 423 ESPP 15% max + lookback. § 6039 Form 3921/3922 required. § 421(b) disqualifying disposition.
view.s444.def.3_months: Maximum 3-month deferral allowed (Sep 30, Oct 31, Nov 30 for calendar required)
view.s444.def.alternative_2: Alternative: shorter than 3 months OK (Oct 31, Nov 30)
view.s444.def.natural_business: Natural Business Year (NBY) exception: no § 7519 required if 25%+ in last 2 months
view.s444.def.partnership: Partnership: must elect by 5/15 of year of election (Form 8716 by 5/15)
view.s444.def.revocation: Revocation: Form 8716 to revert; effective next year
view.s444.def.s_corp: S-corp: within 30 days after Form 2553 election effective date
view.s444.def.short_period_initial: Initial short period: from formation date to elected year-end
view.s444.h1.title: // § 444 FISCAL YEAR ELECTION
view.s444.hint.intro: Partnerships, S-corps, and Personal Service Corps (PSCs) generally must use CALENDAR year (§ 441). § 444 election: up to 3-month deferral (e.g., FY ending Sep 30, Oct 31, Nov 30). Cost: § 7519 required payment (refundable deposit at termination). PSCs: alternative § 280H min salary requirement INSTEAD of § 7519. Natural business year 25%-test alternative: no § 7519 required if 25%+ of gross receipts in last 2 months. Form 8716.
view.s444.label.psc_comp: PSC owner comp pct of services %
view.s444.label.psc_min: PSC min salary paid in deferral ($)
view.s444.nby_note: Natural Business Year (NBY) exception: 25%+ of gross receipts in last 2 months of fiscal year permits SHORT FISCAL YEAR without § 7519 required payment. Common for seasonal businesses (Q4 retail, December construction). No deposit required = no time value lost.
view.s444.req.estate: Estate: any year — typically fiscal in 1st year
view.s444.req.partnership: Partnership: tax year that results in least deferral to partners (usually calendar)
view.s444.req.short_period: Initial / final year: short period return
view.s444.s280h.cure: Pay year-end bonus to cure shortfall
view.s444.s280h.distribution_alternative: Cannot satisfy by distribution alone — must be wages
view.s444.s280h.fail: Failure: deduction limitation on excess of bonus paid during deferral period
view.s444.s280h.formula: Owner salary during deferral months × 365/365 × deferral pct ≥ baseline
view.s444.s280h.policy: Policy goal: ensure PSC owners receive consistent salary throughout fiscal year
view.s444.s280h.purpose: PSC instead of § 7519: minimum salary requirement to owner-employees
view.s444.s280h.timing: Test applied at year-end based on annual salary pattern
view.s444.s7519.cumulative: Cumulative across years — adjusted based on net deferral each year
view.s444.s7519.deposit: Treated as DEPOSIT — refundable when entity terminates / changes year
view.s444.s7519.example: Example: $1M deferral income, 37% rate, 3-month deferral = $1M × 37% × 25% = $92,500 required payment
view.s444.s7519.formula: Required payment = deferral period income × highest rate × (deferred months / 12)
view.s444.s7519.no_interest: NO interest paid on deposit (lost time value)
view.s444.s7519.recovery: Refund: filed within 2 yrs of termination event
view.s446.acc.complex: More complex — A/R, A/P, accruals tracked
view.s446.acc.deductions: Deductions when LIABILITY fixed + amount determinable + economic performance
view.s446.acc.income: Income when EARNED (all-events test + economic performance)
view.s446.acc.tax_shelter: Tax shelter must use accrual regardless of size
view.s446.cash.cap_assets: Capital purchases still depreciated, not expensed
view.s446.cash.constructive: Constructive receipt: available without restriction = received (even if not deposited)
view.s446.cash.eligibility: Eligible: sole prop, partnership, S-corp; C-corp + partnership-with-C-corp if ≤ $30M
view.s446.cash.prepaid: Prepaid expenses can NOT be deducted until benefit consumed (12-month rule limited exception)
view.s446.cash.year_end_planning: Year-end planning: defer income / accelerate expenses
view.s446.h1.title: // § 446 ACCOUNTING METHODS
view.s446.hint.intro: Two primary methods: Cash + Accrual. Hybrid permitted. Cash method generally available if avg gross receipts ≤ $30M (2024) and not 'tax shelter'. C-corps + partnerships with C-corp partner: generally must use accrual if > $30M. Inventory: § 471 generally requires accrual for inventory; small biz exception allows cash basis (§ 471(c)). Method change via Form 3115 + § 481(a) adjustment.
view.s446.hybrid.clearly_reflects: IRS challenges if doesn't clearly reflect income
view.s446.hybrid.combine: Combine cash + accrual for different parts
view.s446.hybrid.common: Common: accrual for inventory + cash for everything else
view.s446.hybrid.farming: Farming + ranching often hybrid (crop insurance + livestock)
view.s4501.cov.derivative: Substantially similar transactions via derivatives — covered (anti-abuse)
view.s4501.cov.expat: Foreign acquirer's US subsidiary repurchases of foreign parent stock — included if reach $1M
view.s4501.cov.private: Excluded: privately held companies (no securities exchange listing)
view.s4501.cov.public: Covered: publicly traded US corp (also 'covered surrogate foreign corp' under § 7874)
view.s4501.cov.tender: Self-tender offers + market repurchases + accelerated share repurchases — all covered
view.s4501.exc.bank_dealer: Bank / Securities Dealer in ordinary course
view.s4501.exc.de_minimis: $1M de minimis: full exemption when annual buybacks ≤ $1M
view.s4501.exc.dividend: Dividend treatment: reduces base by amount treated as dividend by recipient
view.s4501.exc.esop: Contributions to employer-sponsored ESOP: full offset
view.s4501.exc.gh_compete: Non-deductible: § 162 deduction unavailable for excise tax
view.s4501.exc.netting: 'Netting rule': new issuances (compensation + acquisition) reduce base $-for-$
view.s4501.exc.reorg: Reorgs § 368 (qualified): exception when no FMV exchange
view.s4501.exc.spinoff: § 355 spinoffs: distributing corp stock not subject
view.s4501.h1.title: // § 4501 STOCK BUYBACK EXCISE
view.s4501.hint.intro: 1% excise tax on FMV of publicly traded corp stock REPURCHASED, effective after 12/31/2022 (IRA 2022). Net of issuances: NEW stock issued reduces base (compensation, M&A, conversions). Exceptions: dividend treatment, ESOP contributions, ≤ $1M de minimis, RIC/REIT, reorganizations. Pending proposal: rate increase to 4% under various legislative drafts. Filed on Form 7208.
view.s4501.proposal_note: Proposed 4% rate scenario. Various Biden-Harris + congressional proposals quadruple the rate from 1% to 4%. Plan for higher rate by netting more issuances + ESOP contributions.
view.s451.def.afs_method: AFS method: track recognition matching AFS treatment
view.s451.def.cessation: Cessation of business: full inclusion in final year
view.s451.def.deferral_method: Deferral method: defer revenue NOT recognized for book in year of receipt
view.s451.def.full_inclusion: Full inclusion election: irrevocable, include all advance payments in year 1
view.s451.def.non_afs: Non-AFS method: recognize based on tax / book pattern
view.s451.elig.NOT_rent: NOT eligible: rent for real property, royalties for resource extraction, financial instruments
view.s451.elig.eligible_loans: Loan commitments + standby letters of credit
view.s451.elig.eligible_transactions: Eligible transactions: prepayment for future delivery
view.s451.elig.occupancy: Occupancy of space (excl. real property rent)
view.s451.h1.title: // § 451 INCOME + ADVANCE PAYMENTS
view.s451.hint.intro: § 451(b) 'all events test': fixed right to receive + amount determinable + economic performance. § 451(c) (TCJA): advance payments deferred up to 1 year if also deferred for book purposes. Rev. Proc. 2004-34 + 2024 final regs: detailed advance payment rules for goods, services, IP licenses. Full inclusion election: include all advance payments in year-1.
view.s453.caution.depreciation_recap: Depreciation recapture (§ 1245/1250) recognized FULLY in year of sale, NOT installment
view.s453.caution.election_out: Can elect OUT of installment (report all up front) — helpful in loss years
view.s453.caution.interest_charge: Receivables > $150k: § 453A interest charge on deferred tax at AFR-ish rate
view.s453.caution.no_inventory: No installment treatment for inventory or marketable securities
view.s453.caution.related_party: Related-party rule: buyer reselling within 2 years triggers full recognition
view.s453.h1.title: // § 453 INSTALLMENT SALE
view.s453.hint.intro: § 453: recognize gain pro-rata as payments come in. Gross profit % = (sale price − basis − selling expenses) / contract price. Each payment's gain = payment × gross profit %. Receivables > $150k: interest-charge on deferred tax. NOT available for inventory or marketable securities. Useful for real estate + business sales.
view.s457.f_note: § 457(f) ineligible plan: tax at VEST not separation. Plan vesting carefully — accelerated vesting triggers tax even if no distribution. Also subject to § 409A (additional 20% + interest if violation). Common in nonprofit C-suite (university presidents, hospital CEOs, 501(c)(3)).
view.s457.h1.title: // § 457 GOVT / NONPROFIT DEFER COMP
view.s457.hint.intro: § 457(b) ELIGIBLE plan: $23,500 (2025) limit — SAME as § 401(k) but SEPARATE buckets, STACKABLE for combined $47K+ for nonprofit / gov't. Triple catch-up last 3-yr before normal retirement: up to 2× annual limit. § 457(f) INELIGIBLE: top-hat for nonprofits; no $$$ limit but VESTING (substantial risk of forfeiture) required for deferral. Gov't 457(b): rollover allowed; no § 72(t) early penalty. Tax-exempt 457(b): NO rollover; subject to CREDITORS of employer.
view.s457.stack.catch_up: Catch-up available SEPARATELY on each — additional $7,500 (50+) or $11,250 (60-63)
view.s457.stack.dual: Dual employee (gov't day job + nonprofit consulting): can defer FULL limit to BOTH
view.s457.stack.no_double_catch: Triple OR age-50 catch-up — NOT BOTH in same year
view.s457.stack.same_employer: Same employer: § 457(b) deferral SEPARATE from § 401(k) — both at full $23,500
view.s457.tcu.calculation: Limit = MIN(annual limit, deferral underutilized in prior years)
view.s457.tcu.documentation: Document underutilized: track all prior years to plan year
view.s457.tcu.example: Example: 2025 limit $23,500; if underutilized $20K total in prior years → catch-up = $20K
view.s457.tcu.gov_only: Available in BOTH gov't and tax-exempt § 457(b)
view.s457.tcu.no_combine: Cannot combine with age 50+ catch-up in same year
view.s457.tcu.normal_retirement: Normal retirement age: per plan (typically 65, can be earlier)
view.s457.tcu.window: Last 3 years before normal retirement age (as defined in plan)
view.s45Q.bonus_note: 5× bonus multiplier active: prevailing wage + apprenticeship compliance unlocks full $85 / $60 / $180 rate vs $17 / $12 / $36 base. CRITICAL for project economics — documentation + Davis-Bacon Act compliance required throughout construction + 5-yr repair period post-PIS.
view.s45Q.h1.title: // § 45Q CARBON CAPTURE
view.s45Q.hint.intro: IRA 2022 expanded: $85/ton (geologic sequestration), $60/ton (EOR + utilization), $180/ton DAC (Direct Air Capture). Prevailing wage + apprenticeship multiplier 5×: ($17 / $12 / $36). 12-year credit period post-placed-in-service (vs prior 10). § 6417 direct pay for tax-exempts + 5-yr cap for taxable; § 6418 transferability allows credit sale to 3rd parties. Minimum threshold: 1K tons/yr (DAC), 12.5K (industrial), 18.75K (electricity).
view.s45Q.label.price: Market price per ton ($) for transfer
view.s45Q.mon.direct_pay_clawback: Direct pay clawback: 15% / yr if domestic content not met (CCS exception applies)
view.s45Q.mon.direct_pay_eligible: § 6417 direct pay: tax-exempt entities + states + tribal + electric coops fully eligible
view.s45Q.mon.direct_pay_taxable: Taxable entities: § 6417 elective ONLY for first 5 years (CCS, hydrogen, advanced manufacturing)
view.s45Q.mon.market_pricing: Current market: 88-95¢ on $1 for verified projects
view.s45Q.mon.same_or_better: Carbon offset markets bidding up — voluntary carbon markets often above tax credit
view.s45Q.mon.same_year: Election made each year; specified credit amount
view.s45Q.mon.transfer_cash: § 6418 transferability: sell credit for CASH to unrelated buyer at any discount
view.s45Q.mon.transfer_no_recapture: Credit purchaser not subject to recapture if seller in good faith
view.s45Q.thr.contract: Carbon Capture Equipment Contract: written, binding contract
view.s45Q.thr.electricity: Electricity generation: 18,750 tons/year (post-IRA, lower than prior 500K)
view.s45Q.thr.epa: EPA Subpart RR reporting required for geologic sequestration
view.s45Q.thr.lca: Life Cycle Assessment required for utilization (net reduction certification)
view.s45Q.thr.placed: Placed in service before 2033 (originally) — IRA extended to 'construction begin' before Jan 1 2033
view.s45Q.thr.recapture: Recapture: leakage in 3-yr period triggers credit recapture pro-rata
view.s45V.elect.choose: Annual election: choose PTC (§ 45V) OR one-time ITC (§ 48)
view.s45V.elect.fundamental: Fundamental tradeoff: certainty (ITC) vs ongoing (PTC); break-even ~5-6 years
view.s45V.elect.itc_appeal: ITC better when: high capex, low utilization, or quick monetization preferred
view.s45V.elect.itc_recapture: ITC recapture risk if facility sold w/in 5 yrs
view.s45V.elect.ptc_appeal: PTC better when: high utilization (8000+ hrs/yr), low capex relative to output
view.s45V.elect.ptc_no_recapture: PTC: no recapture, but credit only earned in years producing qualifying H2
view.s45V.elect.tax_equity: Tax equity market: both monetizable via § 6418 transferability
view.s45V.full_credit_note: Lowest tier achieved: full $3/kg PTC. Requires either truly renewable electrolysis with three pillars OR SMR + 95%+ CCS + appropriate LCA. Verify with verified third-party LCA + GREET (DOE) model. Maintain throughout 10-yr period — re-verify if pathways change.
view.s45V.h1.title: // § 45V CLEAN HYDROGEN
view.s45V.h2.rates: Credit by GHG intensity (with wage + apprentice 5×)
view.s45V.hint.intro: Tiered by lifecycle GHG emissions: ≤ 0.45 kg CO2e/kg H2 = $3.00/kg; 0.45-1.5 = $1.00/kg; 1.5-2.5 = $0.75/kg; 2.5-4.0 = $0.60/kg. 10-year credit period from facility placed in service. Construction begin before 1/1/2033. 5× wage + apprenticeship multiplier — base rates 1/5 of above. Three pillars regulation (incrementality + temporal matching + deliverability) under 2024 final regs. § 6417 direct pay + § 6418 transferability. § 48 ITC election: may elect § 48 instead.
view.s45V.label.ghg: Lifecycle GHG kg CO2e / kg H2
view.s45V.path.biogas: Biogas reforming: anaerobic digestion of waste → H2; LCA varies widely
view.s45V.path.byproduct: Byproduct hydrogen: from chlor-alkali, steam reforming — may qualify partial
view.s45V.path.electrolysis: Electrolysis (green): renewable electricity + water → H2 + O2; lowest GHG if renewable
view.s45V.path.gas_no_ccs: Conventional SMR (gray): > 9 kg CO2e/kg — NO § 45V credit
view.s45V.path.methane_pyrolysis: Methane pyrolysis (turquoise): solid carbon byproduct; potentially low-LCA
view.s45V.path.nuclear: Nuclear (pink/red): high-temp electrolysis or thermochemical from nuclear heat
view.s45V.path.smr_ccs: Steam Methane Reforming + CCS (blue): natural gas + CCS may qualify if 90%+ capture
view.s45V.pillar.43_safe_harbor: Safe harbor: nuclear existing capacity (up to 200 MW per facility / 5% national)
view.s45V.pillar.deliverability: DELIVERABILITY: same EIA region or DOE-defined contiguous area
view.s45V.pillar.epc: EPCs (Energy Attribute Certificates) used to track renewable sourcing
view.s45V.pillar.eu_comparison: EU has similar 'additionality' standards under Renewable Energy Directive III
view.s45V.pillar.exceptions: Carve-outs: hydropower long-term contracts + ERCOT / California state-specific
view.s45V.pillar.incrementality: INCREMENTALITY: electricity from new renewable source (≤ 36 months pre-production)
view.s45V.pillar.temporal: TEMPORAL MATCHING: hourly matching 2030+ (annual matching 2024-2029 phase-in)
view.s45W.elig.business: USED in TRADE OR BUSINESS (Schedule C, F, Form 1120, etc.)
view.s45W.elig.no_minerals: NO critical minerals + battery components requirements
view.s45W.elig.no_resale: NOT acquired for resale (dealer inventory excluded)
view.s45W.elig.qualifying: Qualifying vehicle: BEV / FCV / PHEV with battery ≥ 15 kWh (or 7 kWh light duty)
view.s45W.elig.taxpayer: Acquired by taxpayer for USE OR LEASE
view.s45W.h1.title: // § 45W COMMERCIAL CLEAN VEHICLE
view.s45W.hint.intro: $7,500 (≤ 14,000 lbs GVWR) OR $40,000 (14K+ GVWR — heavy duty). LESSER of: (1) 15% basis (30% if BEV/FCV) OR (2) incremental cost over comparable ICE vehicle. NO income limit, NO MSRP cap, NO domestic assembly — the 'lease loophole' bypassing § 30D limits. Commercial / business use only. Tax-exempts: § 6417 direct pay; taxable: § 6418 transferability. Lease 'loophole': dealer claims § 45W → passes through as lease discount. Form 8936.
view.s45W.lease.audit_risk: Watch IRS audit — must be GENUINE lease (not disguised sale)
view.s45W.lease.bypass_30d: Lessee bypasses ALL § 30D limits: income, MSRP, assembly, minerals/components
view.s45W.lease.high_income: Used by HIGH-INCOME consumers who fail § 30D MAGI limit
view.s45W.lease.kia_hyundai: Particularly used by Kia / Hyundai (assembled in Korea — failed § 30D NA test)
view.s45W.lease.lobby_concerns: UAW + congressional concerns; some attempted closure but unchanged
view.s45W.lease.mechanism: Mechanism: leasing company (dealer / captive finance) claims § 45W
view.s45W.lease.pass_through: Dealer passes credit to lessee via reduced monthly payment or down payment credit
view.s45W.lease_note: Lease structure: dealer (lessor) claims § 45W → passes through as discount. Bypasses § 30D income / MSRP / NA-assembly limits. Major route for HIGH-INCOME consumers and foreign-built vehicles. Verify genuine lease structure (not disguised sale) to maintain legitimate § 45W eligibility.
view.s45X.h1.title: // § 45X ADVANCED MFG CREDIT
view.s45X.hint.intro: Per-unit credit on US production of clean energy components: solar cells / modules / wafers, wind blades / nacelles / towers, battery cells / modules, inverters, critical minerals. Phases out: 100% through 2029, 75% 2030, 50% 2031, 25% 2032, 0% 2033 (critical minerals stay 100% PERMANENTLY). Sold to unrelated party. § 6417 direct pay (taxable 5-yr / nonprofit perm); § 6418 transferability major monetization. Form 7207.
view.s45X.minerals_note: Critical minerals NEVER phase out — 10% of production costs PERMANENTLY through full IRA horizon. Strategically prioritize lithium, cobalt, graphite, manganese, nickel production in US (39 applicable minerals). Combined with § 30D EV credit's mineral sourcing rules, this drives US battery supply chain re-shoring.
view.s45X.mon.cash_market: Active credit market: 87-94¢ on $1 typical for verified projects
view.s45X.mon.cogs_allocation: COGS allocation reduces taxable income — credit + COGS combo is the planning sweet spot
view.s45X.mon.feedstock_use: Self-use as feedstock: § 45X(c)(2)(C) integrated facility election lets internal use qualify
view.s45X.mon.foreign_entity: FEOC (Foreign Entity of Concern) excluded from claiming + supply chain restrictions
view.s45X.mon.passive_inv: Passive activity rules: § 469 may limit corp partners using PTCs
view.s45X.mon.repurchase: Stockpile + later sale: credit recognized at sale, not production
view.s45X.mon.taxable_5yr: Taxable C-corps: 5-year § 6417 direct pay window starting in production year (2023-2032)
view.s45X.rate.applicable_critical: 39 applicable critical minerals (lithium, cobalt, nickel, manganese, graphite, etc.)
view.s45X.rate.battery_module: Battery module: $10 / kWh ($15 / kWh w/o cells incorporated)
view.s45X.rate.critical_minerals: Critical minerals: 10% of production costs (NEVER phases out)
view.s45X.rate.electrode: Electrode active materials: 10% of production costs
view.s45X.rate.solar_module: Solar module: $0.07 / direct current watt of capacity
view.s45l.h1.title: // § 45L NEW ENERGY EFFICIENT HOME
view.s45l.hint.intro: IRA 2022 redesigned: $2,500 ENERGY STAR single-family / $5,000 ZERH (Zero Energy Ready Home) / multifamily $500-$1,000 per unit ($2,500-$5,000 with prevailing wage + apprenticeship). Builder / eligible contractor claims. Through 12/31/2032. Certification by qualified rater. Form 8908.
view.s45l.who.builder: Eligible contractor: built / produced + sold or leased
view.s45l.who.cert_doc: Certification documentation retained for at least 3 yrs
view.s45l.who.lihtc: LIHTC interaction: § 45L credit may reduce eligible basis
view.s45l.zerh.body: DOE Zero Energy Ready Home: ~40-50% more efficient than typical new construction. Must meet ENERGY STAR + EPA Indoor airPLUS + WaterSense + EPA Renewable Energy Ready Home (RERH) checklist. Renewable energy system (solar) typically required. Targets net-zero energy use when paired with renewable system.
view.s460.ccm_note: CCM (Completed-Contract Method) elected: defer ALL revenue + costs until contract done. Benefit: avoid PCM cash flow on long projects with milestone delays. Cost: no early income; AMT-PCM may still apply for alternative minimum tax purposes.
view.s460.cost.current: Current year revenue = Cumulative revenue − Prior years revenue
view.s460.cost.estimate_change: Estimate change: cumulative catch-up in year of change
view.s460.cost.excludes: Excludes: warranty + post-completion administrative costs from PCM
view.s460.cost.loss_recognize: Anticipated loss: recognize FULLY in current year (no spreading)
view.s460.cost.percent: % Complete = Costs incurred to date / Total estimated costs
view.s460.cost.profit: Gross profit = Revenue recognized − Costs incurred this year
view.s460.cost.subcontracts: Subcontracts: prime contractor still uses PCM on subcontracted portion
view.s460.h1.title: // § 460 LONG-TERM CONTRACTS
view.s460.hint.intro: PCM (Percentage-of-Completion) required for contracts NOT completed in same year started. Cost-to-cost method: revenue = costs incurred / total estimated costs × contract price. Exceptions: small contractor ≤ $30M avg gross receipts + home construction + ≤ 2-yr contracts → Completed-Contract Method (CCM) or cash. 10% method: defer all income/expense until 10% costs incurred. Look-back: Form 8697 — compute actual vs estimated profit; pay / receive interest on differences. § 460(e) certain residential 70/30 split.
view.s460.look.compare: Compare actual final profit to estimated profit at each year
view.s460.look.exception: Exception: small contracts ($1M cumulative, < 2-yr) — opt-out elective
view.s460.look.over: Overpaid tax: receive interest FROM IRS
view.s460.look.purpose: Purpose: prevent abuse of estimates to defer / accelerate tax
view.s460.look.s460_h: § 460(h): Look-back interest method exemption for delayed reapplication
view.s460.look.under: Underpaid tax: pay interest TO IRS
view.s460.req.contracts: Manufacturing contracts: include in PCM if unique items or > 12-mo production
view.s460.req.default: Default: PCM required for contracts not completed in same year started
view.s460.req.services_only: Services-only contracts: NOT § 460 (use general accounting methods)
view.s460.req.small_2yr: Small contractor (≤ $30M avg gross receipts) + ≤ 2-yr contract: CCM allowed
view.s461.ebl.disallowed: Disallowed loss carries forward as NOL
view.s461.ebl.includes_wages: Wages / salary count as business income for EBL test
view.s461.ebl.no_passive: Stacks on top of § 469 passive activity + § 465 at-risk
view.s461.ebl.non_corp: Non-corporate taxpayers only (corps + S-corps not subject)
view.s461.ep.interest: Interest: with passage of time (separate § 461 timing rules)
view.s461.ep.payment_liabilities: Payment-type liabilities (rebates, awards, insurance, workers comp): when PAID
view.s461.ep.taxes_assessed: Real property + payroll taxes: ratably accrued
view.s461.ep.use_property: Use of property: ratably over period of use
view.s461.h1.title: // § 461 ECONOMIC PERFORMANCE
view.s461.hint.intro: All-events test: (1) all events fix liability, (2) amount determinable, (3) economic performance. Economic performance varies: services received, property used, payment for liability. § 461(h)(3) Recurring item exception: recurring liabilities accrue before EP if paid within 8.5 months after year-end. § 461(l) Excess Business Loss limit: $305k/$610k 2024.
view.s461.re.cash_method: Cash method always uses when-paid; this is accrual-method election
view.s461.re.elect: Election made first year + consistent thereafter
view.s461.re.material: Either: not material OR matches income better in current year
view.s461.re.not_payment_liability: Doesn't apply to payment liabilities (rebates, prizes, workers comp, insurance, etc.)
view.s461.re.paid_8_5: Liability paid within 8.5 months after year-end (or with extension)
view.s461l.h1.title: // § 461(l) EXCESS BUSINESS LOSS
view.s461l.hint.intro: Non-corporate excess business loss limit: $305K single / $610K MFJ (2024). Excess = NOL carryforward. Extended through 2028 by OBBBA.
view.s467.app.increasing: Stepped rent: increasing in amount across periods
view.s467.app.long_term: Long-term lease: > 75% of property useful life triggers CRA
view.s467.app.prepaid: Prepaid rent: amount not for current period
view.s467.app.public_lease: Pub. lease (LPI under state law): § 467 may not apply
view.s467.cra.calc: Calculate: present-value level annuity over lease term, AFR applied
view.s467.cra.disqualified: 'Disqualified' lease (sale-leaseback / tax-avoidance): CRA forced even if not stepped
view.s467.cra.gaap: GAAP straight-line lease accounting often aligns w/ CRA (with adjustments)
view.s467.cra.imputed: Difference between CRA and cash payment = IMPUTED INTEREST (separate income / deduction)
view.s467.cra.lessee: Lessee: rent expense at CRA; interest expense on imputed (subject to § 163(j))
view.s467.cra.lessor: Lessor: rent income at CRA; interest income on imputed
view.s467.cra.report: Report this LEVEL amount per year regardless of payments
view.s467.ex.balloon: Balloon payment at end: CRA forces level accrual; large interest imputed
view.s467.ex.cam_charges: CAM (Common Area Maintenance): typically excluded from § 467 (operating exp)
view.s467.ex.gross_up: Gross-up rent provisions: treated as additional rent obligation
view.s467.ex.holiday_period: Initial rent holiday (60-90 days): defer + spread on CRA
view.s467.ex.master_lease: Master lease + ancillary: each component analyzed separately
view.s467.ex.percentage_rent: Percentage rent (retail): § 467 if minimum + sales-based escalators
view.s467.ex.rent_increases: Annual 3% rent increases — typical stepped pattern (CPI escalation)
view.s467.ex.tenant_improvements: Tenant improvement allowance + free rent month: § 467 prepaid analysis
view.s467.h1.title: // § 467 DEFERRED / STEPPED RENT
view.s467.hint.intro: Required for rental property agreements ≥ $250K total rent + deferred / front-loaded / stepped rents. Constant Rental Accrual (CRA): tax follows economic accrual, not cash payments. Time-value-of-money imputed interest applied. Disqualified leaseback OR lease term > 75% useful life triggers harsher CRA mandatory. Forms: Schedule E (real estate) / Schedule K (partnership) + § 467 statement. Aligns lessor + lessee timing.
view.s467.label.useful: Lease term as % of useful life
view.s467.long_note: Long-term lease (> 75% useful life) OR sale-leaseback: CRA mandatory regardless of rent pattern. Plan for level accrual + imputed interest income / expense. Coordinate with § 163(j) interest limit on lessee side. AFR rate impacts deferral cost significantly.
view.s469.aggregation.body: If you have multiple rentals, file an aggregation election treating ALL as ONE activity. Then 750-hr / 100-hr / material participation tests applied across aggregate, not per property. ELECTION IS IRREVOCABLE absent material change of facts. Attach statement to year-1 return. Without this, each property tested separately and material participation often fails.
view.s469.case.agarwal: Agarwal v. Comm'r (2009) — DENIED: physician spouse not REP, >50% in medicine
view.s469.case.bailey: Bailey v. Comm'r (2001) — DENIED: full-time IBM engineer, can't satisfy >50% test
view.s469.case.hakkak: Hakkak v. Comm'r (2020) — DENIED: contemporaneous log lacking, 'ballpark guesses'
view.s469.case.lapid: Lapid v. Comm'r (2017) — DENIED: failed material participation per activity test
view.s469.case.miller: Miller v. Comm'r (2010) — GRANTED: full-time builder, kept time log, 1000+ hrs
view.s469.h1.title: // § 469 PASSIVE ACTIVITY + REP
view.s469.hint.intro: Passive losses only offset passive income — excess SUSPENDED indefinitely until activity disposition or future passive income absorbs. Real Estate Professional (REP): ≥ 750 hrs/yr in real estate trades AND > 50% of personal services in real estate. REP rentals lose passive presumption → potentially unlimited offset against ordinary income (still need material participation per activity, or aggregation election).
view.s469.label.hours_rentals: Your hours in real estate this year
view.s469.label.mp_only_one: You did substantially all the work?
view.s469.warning.self_rental: Self-rental trap: under § 469 net rental INCOME from property leased to your own active business is RECHARACTERIZED as non-passive — cannot absorb other passive losses. Net rental LOSS stays passive. Don't let your CPA put the property in the same entity.
view.s47.h1.title: // § 47 HISTORIC REHAB
view.s47.hint.intro: 20% credit on Qualified Rehabilitation Expenditures (QREs) for CERTIFIED HISTORIC structures. TCJA 2017: 10% credit (pre-1936 non-certified) ELIMINATED. 5-year vesting: claim 4% per year over 5 years (vs prior single-year). NPS / SHPO 3-part app: Part 1 evaluation of significance, Part 2 description of rehab, Part 3 certification. Substantial rehab test: QREs > greater of $5K or building basis. 5-yr recapture on sale or change of use. § 6418 transferability. Form 3468.
view.s47.process.appeal: Appeals: 30 days to NPS within Washington
view.s47.process.fee: User fees: $0-$5,000+ depending on project size
view.s47.process.nps: NPS (National Park Service): final approving authority
view.s47.process.part1: Part 1 — Evaluation of Significance: confirms certified historic structure
view.s47.process.part2: Part 2 — Description of Rehabilitation: review rehabilitation plans BEFORE work
view.s47.process.part3: Part 3 — Request for Certification of Completed Work: AFTER project finished
view.s47.process.shpo: SHPO (State Historic Preservation Officer): initial reviewer in most states
view.s47.process.timeline: Typical timeline: 6-9 months pre-construction; 6 months post-completion
view.s47.qre.depreciable: Depreciable structural improvements: walls, floors, roofs, plumbing, electric, HVAC
view.s47.qre.exterior: Exterior preservation to historic standard (per Secretary of Interior Standards)
view.s47.qre.interior: Interior finish work to historic standard
view.s47.qre.no_acquisition: EXCLUDES: acquisition cost of building, land
view.s47.qre.no_developer_above: EXCLUDES: developer fees in excess of arm's-length
view.s47.qre.no_landscape: EXCLUDES: landscaping, site work, demolition (with some exceptions)
view.s47.rec.basis_reduction: Basis reduction: 100% of credit reduces building basis
view.s47.rec.exception: Casualty + restoration: no recapture if restored to historic standard
view.s47.rec.exempt_lease: Exempt lease > 50% disqualifies (must rent at market to non-exempt)
view.s47.rec.transfer_recapture: Transferability: recapture stays with ORIGINAL TAXPAYER
view.s47.rec.triggers: Triggers: sale, change of use, demolition, change in historic character
view.s47.subst_note: Substantial rehabilitation test FAIL: QREs must exceed greater of $5K OR building basis (excluding land) over 24-60 month measuring period. Phased work over multiple years may require careful planning to meet test.
view.s471.h1.title: // § 471 INVENTORY METHODS
view.s471.hint.intro: Required when production / purchase + sale of merchandise = income factor. Small business exemption: ≤ $30M avg gross receipts may use cash method + treat inventory as non-incidental supplies. Cost-flow methods: FIFO (default), LIFO (§ 472 election), Specific ID, Weighted Average. LIFO requires book conformity + LIFO recapture if S-corp converts from C.
view.s471.lifo.cons: Cons: lower reported earnings; book conformity rule (§ 472(c)); complex layers
view.s471.lifo.dollar_value: Dollar-value LIFO: aggregates by index pool
view.s471.lifo.global_repeal: Global IFRS doesn't allow LIFO (some companies repeal US LIFO too)
view.s471.lifo.pros: Pros: lower COGS in rising prices = lower current taxes; better cash flow
view.s471.lifo.recapture: § 1363(d) LIFO recapture: 4-year recapture on C→S conversion
view.s471.lifo.repeal: Often repealed when prices flat / falling (LIFO liquidation triggers)
view.s471.lifo.simplified: Simplified LIFO available for retailers (BLS index)
view.s471.lifo.unicap: § 263A UNICAP rules add complexity for LIFO
view.s471.sbe.cash_method: Can use cash method even with inventory
view.s471.sbe.deduct_when_paid: Deduct cost in year PAID + provided for sale/use
view.s471.sbe.elect: Election made on Form 3115 with § 481(a) adjustment
view.s471.sbe.no_unicap: No write-down or LCM required either
view.s471.sbe.non_incidental: Inventory treated as non-incidental materials and supplies
view.s472.bc.basic: REQUIREMENT: use LIFO for FINANCIAL reporting too (GAAP / IFRS)
view.s472.bc.consequence: Violation: § 472(c) revokes LIFO election + retroactive FIFO
view.s472.bc.cost_method: 'Cost method' — historical cost basis for LIFO
view.s472.bc.cumulative_only: Disclosure of LIFO Reserve in financial statements: REQUIRED + ALLOWED
view.s472.bc.ifrs: IFRS conversion: IFRS PROHIBITS LIFO — forces LIFO termination if going IFRS
view.s472.bc.parent_sub: Consolidated reporting: each entity's election counts separately
view.s472.bc.terminate: Termination: § 472(e) — recapture LIFO reserve over 3-4 years (Form 3115 method change)
view.s472.h1.title: // § 472 LIFO INVENTORY METHOD
view.s472.hint.intro: LIFO election: identify ending inventory at COST of most recent acquisitions. During inflation: HIGHER COGS + LOWER taxable income vs FIFO. Book conformity rule (§ 472(c)): financial reporting MUST also use LIFO. Dollar-Value LIFO: aggregates similar items into pools by index. LIFO recapture (§ 1363(d)): 4-year recapture on C-corp to S-corp conversion. § 472(e): termination of LIFO method. Form 970 filed to elect. IFRS does NOT allow LIFO (global comparison).
view.s472.mech.cogs: COGS = most recent purchases (top of LIFO stack)
view.s472.mech.dollar_value: Dollar-Value LIFO: aggregate by FMV index (less complex tracking)
view.s472.mech.ending: Ending inventory: older layers preserved (potentially decades old)
view.s472.mech.layers: 'Layers': each year's purchases at year-end cost; oldest at bottom
view.s472.mech.lifo_reserve: LIFO Reserve: difference between FIFO and LIFO inventory = deferred income
view.s472.mech.liquidation: LIFO liquidation: dipping into old layers releases low-cost inventory → income spike
view.s472.mech.simplified_dollar: Simplified Dollar-Value LIFO: BLS / IPIC index allowed for smaller business
view.s472.mech.specific_goods: Specific goods LIFO: track each item type separately
view.s472.no_conformity_note: Book conformity rule VIOLATED — § 472(c) requires LIFO for financial reporting too. IRS may revoke LIFO election retroactively, requiring FIFO recomputation + interest + penalties on understated tax. Disclose LIFO Reserve in financial statement notes; don't show 'as-if FIFO' inventory on main balance sheet.
view.s472.rec.aging_inventory: Common in long-established businesses with significant LIFO reserves
view.s472.rec.basic: C-corp using LIFO converting to S-corp: 4-yr recapture of LIFO Reserve
view.s472.rec.estate_planning: Estate planning: consider LIFO recapture before S-election + heirs
view.s472.rec.formula: Recapture amount = LIFO Reserve = FIFO basis - LIFO basis
view.s472.rec.installments: Section 1378 installment provisions: pay over 4 yrs interest-free
view.s472.rec.partial_election: Partial S-election (split between active + holding) may reduce trigger
view.s472.rec.payments: 25% recapture in C-corp final year + 25% × 3 yrs by S-corp
view.s472.rec.recent_examples: Recent: oil + gas + heavy industry with old LIFO layers
view.s475.h1.title: // § 475 TRADER MARK-TO-MARKET ELECTION
view.s475.hint.intro: § 475(f) qualified trader MTM election: gain/loss ordinary + NO § 1091 wash sale + Schedule C above-line expenses. Election due April 15 unextended.
view.s48.bonus.allocation_required: LMI bonus requires PRE-APPROVAL via Treasury / DOE Capacity Allocation Program
view.s48.bonus.base: BASE rate: 6% (without wage / apprentice OR ≥ 1 MW)
view.s48.bonus.domestic: +10% Domestic Content: ≥ 40% steel + iron domestic; manuf'd products % varies
view.s48.bonus.energy: +10% Energy Community: brownfield / fossil-fuel community / unemployment census
view.s48.bonus.full: FULL rate: 30% (with wage + apprentice OR under 1 MW)
view.s48.bonus.lmi: +10-20% LMI bonus: low-income census tract or affordable housing — competitive allocation
view.s48.bonus.under_1mw: < 1 MW projects: full 30% rate WITHOUT wage / apprentice compliance required
view.s48.h1.title: // § 48 INVESTMENT TAX CREDIT
view.s48.hint.intro: 30% credit on qualified energy property basis (solar, geothermal, fuel cell, wind ≤ 100kW, biogas, CHP, micro-turbine, etc.). 5× bonus structure: base 6% + 24% prevailing wage / apprenticeship multiplier. Adders: +10% domestic content + 10% energy community + 10% LMI bonus (low-income community). Post-2024: § 48E 'Clean Electricity ITC' replaces. § 6417 direct pay tax-exempt; § 6418 transferability taxable. Forms 3468 + 3800.
view.s48.rec.basis_reduce: Basis reduction: 50% of credit (separate basis reduction for adders)
view.s48.rec.casualty_exception: Casualty replacement: no recapture if rebuilt within rules
view.s48.rec.exit_planning: Hold 5+ yrs to avoid recapture — common in tax equity partnerships
view.s48.rec.recapture_form: Form 4255 to compute recapture if triggered
view.s48.rec.transferability: Transferability: recapture stays w/ ORIGINAL TAXPAYER (seller); buyer protected
view.s48.rec.triggers: Triggers: sale, abandonment, theft, casualty, lease change, change of use
view.s48.s48e.45y_ptc: § 45Y is PTC counterpart (operating credit per kWh)
view.s48.s48e.choice: Choose § 48 (legacy) OR § 48E (post-2024) — projects in service 2025+ default to § 48E
view.s48.s48e.future: Long-term: extends until US grid hits 25% of 2022 emissions
view.s48.s48e.phase_in: Begins 2025; § 48 phases out for new projects
view.s48.s48e.phaseout: Phase-out: 100% credit until 2032; phases out as GHG goals met
view.s48.s48e.tech_neutral: Technology-neutral: any zero-emission electricity generation qualifies
view.s481.cc.dcn123: DCN 123: § 461(h) economic performance recurring item exception
view.s481.cc.dcn235: DCN 235: De minimis tangible property election (§ 263(a))
view.s481.cc.dcn7_overall: DCN 7 overall method change automatic if < $25M gross receipts
view.s481.h1.title: // § 481(a) ACCOUNTING METHOD CHANGE
view.s481.hint.intro: Form 3115. § 481(a) adjustment captures difference between old + new method. Positive (increases income): spread over 4 yrs OR year of change for de minimis < $50k. Negative (decreases income): fully in year of change. Automatic (Rev. Proc. 2024-14): no IRS consent, file with return. Non-automatic: advance consent, $11,500 user fee, 6-month review.
view.s481.proc.consistency: Must use new method consistently going forward
view.s481.proc.duplicate: Send duplicate copy to IRS National Office Ogden, UT (since 2024)
view.s481.proc.non_automatic: Non-automatic: $11,500 user fee + advance consent (6 mo review)
view.s481.proc.notice_2022_36: Notice 2022-36 disaster relief: timely filing extension
view.s481.proc.signature: Officer / partner / sole owner signs Form 3115
view.s481.proc.timing: File Form 3115 with timely-filed return (incl. extensions) for year of change
view.s481.proc.under_examination: Under audit / examination: Form 3115 generally NOT permitted (limited exceptions)
view.s482.apa.body: Multi-year prospective agreement with IRS on transfer pricing methodology. Three types: Unilateral (IRS only), Bilateral (US + 1 foreign country), Multilateral (US + multiple). 5-year term typical, renewable. Strong protection from § 6662 penalties + foreign disputes (competent authority). User fee $113,500 (small business reduced).
view.s482.doc.background: Background documents: contracts, transfer-pricing studies, agreements
view.s482.doc.economic: Economic analysis: method selection + comparable selection
view.s482.doc.org_chart: Organization chart + comp data + related party identification
view.s482.doc.overview: Overview of business: organizational structure, ownership, operations, market
view.s482.h1.title: // § 482 TRANSFER PRICING
view.s482.hint.intro: IRS may allocate income / deductions among related parties to clearly reflect income. Standards: arm's-length transactions, comparability + best method analysis. Methods: CUP, RPM, CPM, PSM, TNMM. § 6662(e) penalty: 20% substantial / 40% gross misstatement if no contemporaneous documentation. Six principal documents required for protection. APA (Advance Pricing Agreement) provides certainty.
view.s483.afr.applicable_rate: 'Applicable rate' used to test adequate interest
view.s483.afr.long: Long-term AFR: applies to debt > 9 years (long bond yields)
view.s483.afr.mid: Mid-term AFR: applies to debt > 3 to ≤ 9 years (3-9 year Treasury)
view.s483.afr.published: Published monthly in Rev. Rul. (e.g., Rev. Rul. 2024-X)
view.s483.afr.short: Short-term AFR: applies to debt ≤ 3 years (90-day Treasury)
view.s483.coord.estate: Estate planning: low-AFR sale to grantor trust (private annuity sale)
view.s483.coord.s1239: § 1239 related party: combine with imputed interest analysis
view.s483.coord.s1274: § 1274 debt instruments: applies to PUBLICLY TRADED debt (not § 483)
view.s483.coord.s2032a: § 2032A estate freezes use installment notes with AFR
view.s483.coord.s453: § 453 installment method: imputed interest part NOT subject to installment
view.s483.coord.s453_b_g: § 453B(g): transferred installment notes to spouse — tax-free under § 1041
view.s483.coord.s7702: § 7702 life insurance contracts: separate test, not § 483
view.s483.coord.s7872: § 7872 below-market loans: similar imputation for non-sale loans
view.s483.exc.cash_method: Cash method seller + small amounts: may opt out
view.s483.exc.de_minimis: De minimis: aggregate ≤ $10K sales of property in a year
view.s483.exc.publicly_traded: Publicly traded property: § 1274 may apply instead
view.s483.exc.small_personal: Small personal use property: ≤ $250K sales (e.g., cars)
view.s483.h1.title: // § 483 IMPUTED INTEREST
view.s483.hint.intro: Installment sales with inadequate interest: portion of each payment recharacterized as INTEREST. Test rate: AFR (Applicable Federal Rate) based on term + compounding period. Below-AFR loans: portion of each payment = interest (not capital gain). § 7872 below-market loans + § 1274 debt instruments coordinate. 3 AFR rates: short-term (≤ 3 yrs), mid-term (3-9 yrs), long-term (> 9 yrs). De minimis: ≤ $10K sales without imputed interest.
view.s483.imp.allocation: Allocate to interest first, then to principal recovery
view.s483.imp.below: If stated rate < AFR: portion of each payment = imputed interest
view.s483.imp.buyer_basis: Buyer's basis = principal portion (NOT total payments)
view.s483.imp.character: Interest portion: ORDINARY INCOME for seller, deductible (§ 163) for buyer
view.s483.imp.formula: Imputed interest = AFR × outstanding balance per period
view.s483.imp.principal: Principal portion: capital gain (per § 453 installment method)
view.s483.imp.section_1274: § 1274 may apply instead for 'publicly traded' debt instruments
view.s483.imp.test: Test: stated rate ≥ AFR → adequate interest; no imputation
view.s483.related_note: BELOW-AFR + RELATED PARTY: § 483 imputed interest combined w/ § 7872 below-market loan rules + § 1239 related party rules. IRS scrutiny on private installment sales among family. Document at-least-AFR stated rate. Estate planning structure (intra-family sales) requires careful AFR documentation + annual interest reporting.
view.s48C.h1.title: // § 48C ADVANCED ENERGY
view.s48C.hint.intro: Competitive allocation by DOE: 30% ITC (with wage + apprenticeship) on energy property + advanced manufacturing equipment. Reauthorized IRA 2022 with $10B (40% reserved for energy communities). Round 1: $4B (Mar 2023); Round 2: $6B (Jan 2024). Reserved for: clean energy manufacturing + critical minerals + industrial decarbonization. 2-yr placed-in-service after allocation. Forms 3468 + 3800 + DOE concept paper + full app. § 6418 transferability allowed; NO § 6417 direct pay for § 48C.
view.s48C.no_alloc_note: NO § 48C credit without DOE allocation. Apply at energy.gov 48C-PORTAL: 8-page concept paper → encouragement letter → 30-page full application → allocation. Process takes 6-9 months. Consider § 48 ITC (automatic, no allocation) as alternative.
view.s48C.proc.allocation: Step 4: DOE selects + Treasury allocates credit amount
view.s48C.proc.encourage: Step 2: DOE 'Encouragement Letter' — proceed with full application
view.s48C.proc.evaluation_criteria: Evaluation: (1) commercial viability, (2) job creation, (3) GHG reduction, (4) supply chain diversification
view.s48C.proc.full_app: Step 3: 30-page full application + technical details + commercial plan
view.s48C.proc.placed: Step 5: Place in service within 2 years of allocation (extension up to 1 yr)
view.s48C.proc.recapture: Step 7: 5-yr recapture period (sale, abandonment, change of use)
view.s48C.round.beats_45x: Stacking: § 48C + § 45X possible if separate qualifying expenditures (different basis)
view.s48C.round.r1_amount: Round 1: $4B allocated (Mar 2023); 80 projects selected from 250+ applicants
view.s48C.round.r2_energy: Round 2: $2.5B for energy communities + 80% domestic content bonus
view.s48C.round.r3_pending: Round 3: pending congressional authorization for additional funding
view.s48C.round.success_rate: Award rate: ~30-40% of full applicants — competitive
view.s48C.round.timing: Timing: concept papers due ~Q1; full applications ~Q3; allocations ~Q4-Q1 next yr
view.s4940.ded.necessary: Other ordinary + necessary expenses related to income production
view.s4940.ded.no_charitable: NOT charitable program expenses (those go in Part I-A)
view.s4940.ded.tax: State + local property taxes on investment real estate
view.s4940.est.body: If § 4940 tax expected to exceed $500, quarterly estimated payments required by 5/15, 6/15, 9/15, 12/15. Form 990-W for computation. Safe harbor: 100% prior year OR 90% current year. Underpayment penalty under § 6655.
view.s4940.h1.title: // § 4940 PF NET INVESTMENT INCOME
view.s4940.hint.intro: 1.39% flat tax on PF net investment income (interest, dividends, royalties, rents, net capital gains). Pre-2020 was 2% with reduced 1% rate available if distribution test met — SECURE Act eliminated 2-tier, made flat 1.39%. Reported on Form 990-PF Part XI. Net long-term capital losses do NOT offset other income (PF specific). Quarterly estimated tax payments required if > $500.
view.s4940.inc.cap_gain: Net long-term capital gains (cap losses do NOT offset NII)
view.s4940.inc.short_term: Short-term capital gains (treated as ordinary income)
view.s4941.dq.35_owned: Entity 35%+ owned by combination of disqualified persons
view.s4941.dq.family: Family member (spouse, ancestor, descendant + their spouses)
view.s4941.dq.substantial: Substantial contributor (≥ $5k AND > 2% of total contributions historically)
view.s4941.ex.bank_general: Bank deposit accounts at general commercial terms
view.s4941.ex.incidental: Incidental benefit from charitable activities (e.g., banquet seat)
view.s4941.ex.no_charge: Free use of property by foundation
view.s4941.ex.no_interest_loan: Interest-free / no-interest loans TO foundation (from DP)
view.s4941.ex.reasonable_comp: Reasonable comp for personal services NECESSARY to foundation
view.s4941.h1.title: // § 4941 PF SELF-DEALING
view.s4941.hint.intro: Private foundation can't transact with 'disqualified persons' (substantial contributor, manager, family, 35% owned entity). STRICT LIABILITY — no intent required. 10% on disqualified person + 5% on knowing manager INITIAL. 200% on DP + 50% on manager if not corrected within taxable period. Even 'fair price' transactions PROHIBITED. Form 4720 + Form 990-PF.
view.s4941.rel.4945: § 4945: 20% on taxable expenditures (lobbying, political, individual grants)
view.s4942.cf.fifo: FIFO usage: oldest carryover absorbed first
view.s4942.cf.set_aside: Set-aside elections require IRS approval + 5-yr limit
view.s4942.cf.transition: Newly-converted PF: 4-yr transition rule for full payout requirement
view.s4942.cf.unusual_grant: Unusual grant exception (Rev. Proc. 76-13) for one-time mega contributions
view.s4942.h1.title: // § 4942 PF UNDISTRIBUTED INCOME
view.s4942.hint.intro: PF must distribute 5% of average non-charitable-use asset value annually for charitable purposes. 30% initial excise on undistributed + 100% if not corrected within taxable period. Carryforward 5 yrs. Form 990-PF Part XII. Operating foundations exempt (substantially-all assets directly used in charitable activities).
view.s4942.qual.amounts_paid: Amounts paid OR set aside for charitable purposes
view.s4942.qual.daf_no: Grants to DAFs DO count (since SECURE 2.0)
view.s4942.qual.direct: Direct charitable activities (educational programs, research, museum operations)
view.s4942.qual.exempt_purpose: Acquisition of property for exempt purposes
view.s4942.qual.foreign: Grants to foreign organizations with equivalency determination
view.s4942.qual.grants: Grants to public charities + foreign organizations (with expenditure responsibility)
view.s4942.qual.set_aside: Set-asides for specific future projects (5-yr limit + IRS approval)
view.s4943.disp.pri_conversion: Re-characterize as PRI through governance change (Newman 2022)
view.s4943.disp.sell_market: Sell to non-DP in arm's-length market transaction
view.s4943.disp.spin_off: Spin off the excess holdings to operating subsidiary
view.s4943.ex.de_minimis: De minimis: PF alone ≤ 2% always allowed regardless of aggregate
view.s4943.ex.passive_income: Passive income business (rents, royalties, etc.) — different rules
view.s4943.ex.secure_2_0: SECURE 2.0 Newman exception: solely-owned biz qualifies as PRI if all profits charity
view.s4943.ex.unrelated_business: Unrelated trade or business: not exempt; subject to UBTI
view.s4943.h1.title: // § 4943 PF EXCESS BUSINESS HOLDINGS
view.s4943.hint.intro: PF + disqualified persons may hold up to 20% of voting stock (35% if 3rd-party effective control). De minimis: PF alone ≤ 2% always permitted. 5-yr divestiture period for gifts / bequests (extendable). 10% initial excise on excess + 200% if not corrected within taxable period. SECURE 2.0: 100% solely-owned biz holdings can qualify as Program-Related Investment (PRI).
view.s4944.h1.title: // § 4944 PF JEOPARDIZING INVESTMENTS
view.s4944.hint.intro: PF managers must exercise ordinary business care + prudence — investments jeopardizing charitable purpose = excise. 10% initial on PF + 10% on manager (knowing). 25% PF + 5% additional manager if not corrected. § 4944(c) PRI EXEMPT — primary purpose charitable + no significant income purpose + no lobbying / political. Most foundations use modern portfolio theory.
view.s4944.pri.counts_payout: PRIs COUNT toward § 4942 5% minimum payout requirement
view.s4944.pri.examples: Examples: micro-loans, low-interest community development loans, charter school bonds
view.s4944.pri.no_income: No SIGNIFICANT purpose to produce income
view.s4944.pri.no_political: No purpose to influence legislation / political campaigns
view.s4944.pri.primary: Primary purpose: accomplish exempt purposes (charitable / educational / scientific)
view.s4944.pri.terms_loans: Below-market loans + equity in social enterprises common PRIs
view.s4944.prud.long_short: Long-term + short-term financial needs of PF
view.s4944.prud.no_per_se: NO per se prohibited investments under modern prudent investor rule
view.s4944.prud.review: Periodic portfolio review by qualified advisors
view.s4944.prud.uniform_act: Uniform Prudent Management of Institutional Funds Act (UPMIFA) — state law overlay
view.s4945.er.cost_segregation: Grantee separately accounts for grant funds
view.s4945.er.pre_grant: Pre-grant inquiry: ensure grantee + funds will be used per grant terms
view.s4945.er.records: PF retains records demonstrating proper use
view.s4945.er.recovery: Take appropriate steps if funds misused (refund / lawsuit)
view.s4945.er.report_to_irs: PF reports grants under expenditure responsibility on Form 990-PF Schedule O
view.s4945.er.written: Written grant agreement specifying charitable use
view.s4945.h1.title: // § 4945 PF TAXABLE EXPENDITURES
